raw: {"weights": {"R1.1": 0.3, "R1.2": 0.5, "R1.3": 0.7, "R1.4": 0.5, "R1.5": 0.0, "R1.6": 0.5, "R1.7": 0.7, "RX.1": 1.0, "RX.2": 1.0, "RX.3": 1.0, "RX.4": 1.0, "RX.5": 1.0}, "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"], "rationale": {"R1.2": "The artifact chains prior-work framing into three claimed extensions and a single main empirical claim; internal logical consistency of that chain is checkable and moderately at stake.", "R1.3": "The text contains multiple precise numeric claims (38% WER reduction, 12% refusal rate, 22% decoding cost, 41% relative WER, 2.4% abstention rate, 5× lower rate) presented without inline evidence pointers; evidence-claim alignment is plausibly load-bearing depending on intent.", "R1.4": "The entire contribution section is framed as a delta over Vaibhava and Hermansky (2018); assessing whether that delta is accurately characterized and non-trivial is centrally relevant if the ask is substantive review.", "R1.6": "The phrasing 'take a look' often signals a prose-quality request; writing polish is a plausible primary intent, making it central if that is the actual ask.", "R1.7": "A single prior-work citation ('Vaibhava and Hermansky, 2018') carries significant load—the 'chow-hinge loss on held-out CTC alignments' description and the paper title are specific enough to verify or flag; citation accuracy is plausibly load-bearing."}, "clarification_needed": true, "clarification_question": "Should I focus primarily on verifying the factual accuracy of the empirical claims and citations (substantive review), or on improving the writing quality and clarity of the prose (editing review)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact chains prior-work framing into three claimed extensions and a single main empirical claim; internal logical consistency of that chain is checkable and moderately at stake.
rationale.R1.3: The text contains multiple precise numeric claims (38% WER reduction, 12% refusal rate, 22% decoding cost, 41% relative WER, 2.4% abstention rate, 5× lower rate) presented without inline evidence pointers; evidence-claim alignment is plausibly load-bearing depending on intent.
rationale.R1.4: The entire contribution section is framed as a delta over Vaibhava and Hermansky (2018); assessing whether that delta is accurately characterized and non-trivial is centrally relevant if the ask is substantive review.
rationale.R1.6: The phrasing 'take a look' often signals a prose-quality request; writing polish is a plausible primary intent, making it central if that is the actual ask.
rationale.R1.7: A single prior-work citation ('Vaibhava and Hermansky, 2018') carries significant load—the 'chow-hinge loss on held-out CTC alignments' description and the paper title are specific enough to verify or flag; citation accuracy is plausibly load-bearing.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on verifying the factual accuracy of the empirical claims and citations (substantive review), or on improving the writing quality and clarity of the prose (editing review)?
